name: company-funnel-rules
description: Company-wide business defaults for generated Web2App funnels. This file owns funnel intent and reusable product-flow preferences only; runtime mechanics live in runtime adapters, contracts, and recipes.

owns: company preferences that product runs may override

overridePolicy.default: Rules are enforced as company defaults unless an explicit product/user requirement overrides them.
overridePolicy.hard: Hard rules should be preserved by default and surfaced by validation when missing.
overridePolicy.overrideAllowed: When true, a product requirement may replace the rule, but generated outputs must record the reason.

rules.description: Every generated Web2App funnel should start with an entry_page portal screen before quiz progress begins. The page should communicate the product promise, show a dominant hero image, offer a start-funnel CTA, and provide a returning-user login route.

An age_group page should appear early after the entry portal because age is a high-value personalization signal. Age ranges should be derived from product audience analysis, not copied as a universal fixed set.
Age group normally uses four scannable options for mobile ergonomics. Product strategy may adjust the ranges, labels, or count when the audience clearly requires it.

Standard fitness funnels should include a trust-building intro_page shortly after age_group. It should use the user's age context to create reassurance, momentum, or relevance without copying competitor wording.

When relevant, pair a current-state question with a desired-state question, such as current body identity and desired body identity, current ability and desired ability, or current discomfort and desired comfort.
Paywall should appear only after the user has seen a believable result bridge, such as summary, analysis, plan generation, or plan ready. The user should feel the offer was shaped from their answers.

After purchase and account creation/login, generated funnels should provide an account center where users can view subscription information and manage cancellation. The entry_page login route should lead to the same account path for returning users.
